name: Alert
description: Inline feedback banner for status messages

props.name: onClose
props.description: Renders a close button when provided

example:
<Alert variant="success" onClose={() => {}}>
  <AlertTitle>Success</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>Your changes have been saved.</AlertDescription>
</Alert>
guidelines: Use the correct variant to match the message type: `success` (nova), `error` (error), `warning` (solar), `info` (aurora).
Don't use Alert for transient messages — use Toast instead.

name: Avatar
description: Round image display with initials fallback

props.name: alt
props.description: Required. Alt text and fallback initial source

example: <Avatar src="/photo.jpg" alt="Jane Doe" size="lg" />
guidelines: Always provide a meaningful `alt` prop — it drives the initials fallback and accessibility.

name: Background
description: Animated visual backgrounds: starfield, galaxy, milky way, gradient, solid

props.name: ribbons
props.description: Number of ribbons for milkyway variant

props.name: showGradient
props.description: Show blob overlay on gradient variant

example: Content over galaxy background
guidelines: Use for hero sections or full-page backgrounds only — not for card or component backgrounds.
Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` — disable `twinkle` if the user prefers reduced motion.

name: Badge
description: Inline label for status, counts, or categories
example: <Badge variant="subtle" color="success">Published</Badge>
guidelines: Don't combine cosmos + nebula + aurora colors in the same component — it looks AI-generated.
Use for status, counts, or categories. Not for decorative purposes.

name: Breadcrumb
description: Accessible breadcrumb navigation with compound components

props.name: isCurrentPage
props.description: BreadcrumbLink: marks current page, renders as span with aria-current

example:
<Breadcrumb>
  <BreadcrumbList>
    <BreadcrumbItem><BreadcrumbLink href="/">Home</BreadcrumbLink></BreadcrumbItem>
    <BreadcrumbSeparator />
    <BreadcrumbItem><BreadcrumbLink isCurrentPage>Docs</BreadcrumbLink></BreadcrumbItem>
  </BreadcrumbList>
</Breadcrumb>
guidelines: Mark the last item with `isCurrentPage` to set `aria-current="page"` for screen readers.
Supports `asChild` on BreadcrumbLink for custom element rendering (e.g., Next.js Link).

name: Button
description: Interactive button with variants, sizes, and loading state

props.name: asChild
props.description: Render child as root (Radix Slot pattern)

example:
<Button variant="solid" size="md">Click me</Button>
<Button variant="outline" loading>Saving...</Button>
guidelines: Use at most one `solid` button per visual group (card, dialog, section). Use `outline` or `ghost` for secondary actions.
Reserve `glow` variant for hero CTAs only — overuse makes UI look AI-generated.
Touch targets must be >= 44x44px. Use `size="lg"` for primary mobile actions.
Supports `asChild` for custom element rendering (e.g., wrapping an anchor tag).

name: Card
description: Content container with optional hover lift effect
example:
<Card hoverable>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Title</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Description</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>Content here</CardContent>
  <CardFooter><Button>Action</Button></CardFooter>
</Card>
guidelines: Use `void-surface` for card backgrounds — not `void-base` (page) or `void-overlay` (floating layers).
Card internal spacing should be smaller than the gap between cards.
Avoid accent color bars + matching glow on every card — reserve accent treatments for one card per group.

name: Carousel
description: Embla-powered content carousel with keyboard support and accessible slide announcements

props.name: loop
props.description: Wrap from the last slide back to the first

props.name: setApi
props.description: Expose the underlying Embla API for advanced control

example: Project one Project two
guidelines: Give the root Carousel an `aria-label` that names the content set for screen readers.
Pair swipe or drag gestures with visible previous / next controls so mouse and keyboard users get the same navigation path.
Keep each slide focused on one primary message — overcrowded interactive slides are harder to navigate and announce clearly.

name: Checkbox
description: Accessible checkbox with optional label, built on Radix UI
example:
<Checkbox label="Accept terms" defaultChecked />
<Checkbox label="Required field" error />
guidelines: Always associate error messages with `aria-describedby` using `React.useId()`.
Use `aria-invalid` when in error state.

name: Dialog
description: Accessible modal dialog with overlay, built on Radix UI
example:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild><Button>Open</Button></DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Are you sure?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>This action cannot be undone.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter><Button>Confirm</Button></DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
guidelines: Use `void-overlay` for dialog panel backgrounds (elevated surface).
Include at most one `solid` action button. Secondary actions should use `outline` or `ghost`.

name: Footer
description: Site footer with grid content layout
example:
<Footer>
  <FooterContent>Links and content</FooterContent>
  <FooterDivider />
  <FooterBottom>© 2024 Stella UI</FooterBottom>
</Footer>

name: Header
description: Flexible site header with sticky, blur, and mobile nav support
example: Stella UI Home Docs
guidelines: `backdrop-filter: blur()` is allowed on Header (navigation chrome) but nowhere else.
Collapse navigation into a hamburger/drawer below `lg` (1024px) using the `mobileNav` prop.

name: Heading
description: Semantic h1–h6 with size, weight, and alignment control
example:
<Heading level={1} size="3xl">Page Title</Heading>
<Heading level={2} align="center" weight="semibold">Section</Heading>
guidelines: Left-align text by default. Center-align only for hero headlines and short labels.
Use `display` or `statement` font families for emphasis only — never for navigation, form labels, or body text.
Supports `asChild` for custom element rendering.

name: Input
description: Single-line text input with size and error state

props.name: error
props.description: Error state; pass string to show message below input

example:
<Input placeholder="Enter email" />
<Input error="Email is required" value="" />
guidelines: Always associate error messages with `aria-describedby` using `React.useId()`.
Use `aria-invalid` when in error state.

name: PageGrid
description: CSS grid layout for full pages with sidebar support
example:
<PageGrid layout="sidebar-left" gap="8">
  <aside>Sidebar</aside>
  <main>Content</main>
</PageGrid>

name: Radio
description: Radio button group, built on Radix UI
example:
<RadioGroup defaultValue="b">
  <RadioItem value="a" label="Option A" />
  <RadioItem value="b" label="Option B" />
</RadioGroup>
guidelines: Always associate error messages with `aria-describedby` using `React.useId()`.
Use `aria-invalid` when in error state.

name: Section
description: Semantic section wrapper with max-width and padding presets
example:
<Section size="lg" padding="lg">
  <Heading level={2}>Features</Heading>
  <Text>Explore what Stella UI offers.</Text>
</Section>
guidelines: Supports `asChild` for custom element rendering.

name: Select
description: Accessible dropdown select, built on Radix UI
example:
<Select>
  <SelectTrigger placeholder="Pick a fruit" />
  <SelectContent>
    <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
    <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
  </SelectContent>
</Select>
guidelines: Always associate error messages with `aria-describedby` using `React.useId()`.
Use `aria-invalid` when in error state.

name: Separator
description: Visual divider between content sections

props.name: decorative
props.description: Hides from screen readers when true

example:
<Separator />
<Separator orientation="vertical" />
guidelines: Set `decorative={false}` when the separator conveys meaningful content boundaries for screen readers.

name: Skeleton
description: Loading placeholder with pulse animation

props.name: lines
props.description: Number of text lines (text variant only)

example:
<Skeleton variant="text" lines={3} />
<Skeleton variant="circular" width={40} height={40} />
guidelines: Match the skeleton shape to the component it replaces — don't use generic rectangles for everything.
Use `circular` for avatars, `text` for paragraphs, and `rectangular` for images or cards.

name: Stack
description: Flexbox layout primitive for vertical or horizontal lists
example:
<Stack direction="horizontal" gap="4" align="center">
  <Avatar alt="U" />
  <Text>Username</Text>
</Stack>
guidelines: Component-internal spacing < component-external spacing.
Supports `asChild` for custom element rendering.

name: Switch
description: Toggle switch, built on Radix UI
example: <Switch label="Enable notifications" defaultChecked />
guidelines: Always associate error messages with `aria-describedby` using `React.useId()`.
Use `aria-invalid` when in error state.

name: Tabs
description: Tabbed content panels, built on Radix UI
example:
<Tabs defaultValue="tab1" variant="line">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="tab1">Overview</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="tab2">Settings</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="tab1">Overview content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="tab2">Settings content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
guidelines: Use `line` variant for content-level tabs and `solid` variant for navigation-level tabs.

name: Text
description: Polymorphic body text primitive
example:
<Text size="lg" weight="medium">Body copy</Text>
<Text as="span" color="secondary" size="sm">Caption</Text>
guidelines: Left-align text by default. Center-align only for hero headlines and short labels.
Use `display` or `statement` font families for emphasis only — never for navigation, form labels, or body text.
Supports `asChild` for custom element rendering.

name: Toast
description: Ephemeral notification, built on Radix UI
example:
// Wrap app once:
<ToastProvider><ToastViewport /></ToastProvider>

// Then render:
<Toast open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} variant="success">
  <ToastTitle>Saved!</ToastTitle>
  <ToastDescription>Your file has been saved.</ToastDescription>
  <ToastClose />
</Toast>
guidelines: Use appropriate `variant` to match the message type: `success` (nova), `error` (error), `warning` (solar), `info` (aurora).
Don't use Toast for persistent or critical messages — use Alert instead.

name: Tooltip
description: Hover tooltip, built on Radix UI
example:
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip>
    <TooltipTrigger asChild><Button>Hover me</Button></TooltipTrigger>
    <TooltipContent side="top">Helpful hint</TooltipContent>
  </Tooltip>
</TooltipProvider>
guidelines: Use for supplementary info only — never for essential content that the user must read.
Always wrap with `TooltipProvider` at the app root level.
